title: Settings: Errors
text: Settings: Errors Set a specific ProcScript error value ( $procerror ) as a breakpoint. How to To add a ProcScript error as a breakpoint: Click Add . Enter a (negative) value of the ProcScript error you want to set as a breakpoint. (The Description is displayed after you have entered an error value and left the field.) Click Apply to save the new breakpoint. To remove a ProcScript error breakpoint, select it and click Remove , then Apply . Related Links Breakpoints A breakpoint is a marked code line, trigger, error, exception, or condition that forces the application or component to break and the debugger to take control of execution. Error Context Displays the contents of $procerrorcontext whenever a ProcScript error occurs ($procerror< 0). Settings: I/O Print Choose the level of I/O messages that are displayed in the message frame or transcript window. Settings: Tracing Choose the level or type of ProcScript-related tracing to be displayed in the message frame or transcript window. Settings: Triggers Choose the triggers you want to be enabled or disabled for debugging, or those that you want to be set as breakpoints. Settings: Message Frame Specify whether the clrmess ProcScript instruction is enabled or disabled, and whether application messages are sent to the application's message frame or to the application's transcript window.
